negative_examples.reason: This is a knowledge question that requires external information, not a tool task. No available tool can fetch real-time weather data.

negative_examples.user_request: Create a Python function that calculates fibonacci
negative_examples.reason: User wants to create file content for a new function. The distractors involve git operations and messaging which are unrelated to creating file content.
This is an explanatory/educational request. The model should answer directly without tool calls, as no tool is needed to explain concepts.

negative_examples.user_request: Search for error handling patterns in Python
negative_examples.reason: User wants to search for patterns in code. While grep is appropriate, read/write are for file content, not searching. Bash could run commands but grep is the direct tool.

negative_examples.user_request: Send a message to my team about the deployment
negative_examples.reason: The correct tool is telegram_send_message for messaging. Git commit is for version control, read_file is for reading, webfetch is for web content - all unrelated to sending messages.
User wants to understand code. Read_file is appropriate. Telegram/GitHub/Obsidian are for messaging, issues, and notes - not for reading arbitrary code files.
User wants to execute tests. Bash can run 'pytest' or similar commands. Read/write/grep are for file operations, not execution.
Git status is a specific operation. The distractors (read/write/message) don't relate to version control status queries.

negative_examples.user_request: Write unit tests for this module
negative_examples.reason: User wants to create new test files. Write_file is correct. Grep searches, bash executes, telegram messages - none create new content.

negative_examples.user_request: Look up the Notion API documentation
negative_examples.reason: User needs to look up documentation. Context7 provides docs. File operations and git commit don't fetch documentation.
Grep searches file contents for patterns. Read/write are for individual files, github_search searches remote repos.

negative_examples.user_request: Create a new branch for my feature
negative_examples.reason: Git branch creates a new branch. File operations and messaging tools don't handle version control.

negative_examples.user_request: Commit these changes with a message
negative_examples.reason: Git commit saves changes with a message. File operations don't commit, webfetch doesn't version control.

negative_examples.user_request: What is the meaning of life?
negative_examples.reason: Philosophical question that the model can answer directly. No tool is needed or would help.
Obsidian is for vault notes. Generic read_file doesn't know about the vault structure. Git commit doesn't read notes.
GitHub search is for remote repo code. Grep is for local, read_file is for specific files, webfetch fetches web pages.
Install requires running package manager commands (npm/pip). Bash executes commands. File operations don't install.
Delete requires rm command via bash. Read and write don't delete. Grep doesn't handle file deletion.

negative_examples.user_request: What files have changed since last commit?
negative_examples.reason: Git diff shows changes. File operations don't show diffs. Grep searches content, not changes.

negative_examples.user_request: Create an issue on GitHub about this bug
negative_examples.reason: GitHub issue creation is specific. File operations don't create issues. Telegram doesn't interact with GitHub.

negative_examples.user_request: Refactor this function to use async/await
negative_examples.reason: Refactoring requires editing code. Read shows code but doesn't change it. Bash executes, telegram messages.

negative_examples.user_request: Find the definition of this function
negative_examples.reason: LSP goto definition navigates to symbol definition. Grep finds text but not definitions. Read/write are for content.

negative_examples.user_request: Rename this variable across the project
negative_examples.reason: LSP rename does safe cross-project renaming. Edit changes one location. Grep finds but doesn't rename.

negative_examples.user_request: Get type errors in this file
negative_examples.reason: LSP diagnostics finds type errors. Grep finds text, read shows content, bash executes - none analyze types.

negative_examples.user_request: List all symbols in this file
negative_examples.reason: LSP symbols lists all symbols. Grep searches patterns, read shows content - neither lists symbols.

negative_examples.user_request: Show me the recent commit history
negative_examples.reason: Git log shows commit history. File operations don't show git history. Grep searches content.

negative_examples.user_request: Check for secrets in the codebase
negative_examples.reason: Search for secrets (API keys, passwords) via grep. File operations don't search. Telegram doesn't help.
Build requires running build commands (npm build, make, etc.). Bash executes. Others don't build.
Formatting runs tools like prettier, black, gofmt. Bash executes these. Others don't format.
Lint runs eslint, pylint, etc. via bash. File operations don't lint. Grep finds text, not lint issues.
Webfetch retrieves web content. File operations read local files. Git commit doesn't fetch URLs.

negative_examples.user_request: Search the web for best practices
negative_examples.reason: Websearch performs web searches. File operations don't search the web. Grep searches local files.
PR creation is specific to GitHub. File operations don't create PRs. Git commit is local only.
GitHub issues listing is specific. File ops don't list issues. Telegram doesn't query GitHub.
GitHub file get fetches from remote repo. Read reads local. Write writes local. Grep searches.

negative_examples.user_request: Append content to my daily note
negative_examples.reason: Obsidian append adds to vault notes. File write overwrites. Bash doesn't interact with Obsidian.
Obsidian search queries vault. Grep searches local files but not vault. File ops don't search.
Complexity analysis runs tools like radon, lizard. Bash executes. Others don't analyze complexity.
Find unused imports via grep for imports. Write creates files. Bash can run tools but grep is direct.

negative_examples.user_request: What are the references to this function?
negative_examples.reason: LSP find references finds all symbol references. Grep finds text but not semantic references.
Start dev server runs npm start, python manage.py, etc. via bash. File ops don't start servers.
Coverage runs coverage tools (pytest-cov, coverage.py). Bash executes. Others don't measure coverage.
Git merge combines branches. File ops don't merge. Bash can run git but merge is specific.
Git pull fetches and merges remote changes. File ops don't pull. Grep doesn't handle git.
Git push sends commits to remote. File ops don't push. Bash can run git but push is specific.
Clone runs git clone via bash. File ops don't clone. Grep doesn't clone repos.
Session list shows past sessions. File ops don't list sessions. Grep doesn't query sessions.
Session search queries session history. File ops don't search sessions. Grep is for files.
Session info shows session metadata. File ops don't query sessions. Bash doesn't get session info.
Memory search queries unified memory. File ops don't search memory. Grep is for files.
Memory write stores to memory system. Read gets from memory but doesn't write. Grep searches.
Memory stats shows memory system stats. File ops don't get stats. Grep doesn't query stats.
Intelligence route recommends agent routing. File ops don't route. Bash doesn't route tasks.
Complexity scoring rates task difficulty. File ops don't score. Grep doesn't assess complexity.
List agents shows available agents. File ops don't list agents. Bash doesn't list agents.
Quality gates run all validation checks. File ops don't run gates. Grep doesn't run gates.
Type check runs TypeScript/Python type checking. File ops don't type check. Grep doesn't type check.
Security scan checks for secrets. File ops don't scan. Grep finds but doesn't scan systematically.

negative_examples.user_request: Think through this problem step by step
negative_examples.reason: Sequential thinking provides chain-of-thought reasoning. File ops don't think. Bash executes commands.

negative_examples.user_request: Create an entity in the knowledge graph
negative_examples.reason: Memory create adds to knowledge graph. File ops don't create graph entities. Grep doesn't create.
Memory search queries knowledge graph. File ops don't query graph. Grep is for text search.

negative_examples.user_request: Who wrote this line of code?
negative_examples.reason: Git blame shows code authorship. File ops don't blame. Grep finds but doesn't attribute.

negative_examples.user_request: Find the commit that introduced this bug
negative_examples.reason: Git bisect finds buggy commits. File ops don't bisect. Grep doesn't find by bug.
Git branch shows current branch. File ops don't show branches. Bash can run git but branch is specific.
Git stash saves uncommitted changes. File ops don't stash. Grep doesn't stash.
Git stash pop applies stashed changes. File ops don't pop stash. Grep doesn't apply.
Git reset reverts to commit. File ops don't reset. Grep doesn't reset commits.
Git rebase reapplies commits. File ops don't rebase. Bash can run git but rebase is specific.
Git cherry-pick applies single commit. File ops don't cherry-pick. Grep doesn't pick commits.
Git squash combines commits. File ops don't squash. Bash can run but squash is specific.
